i18n_tool_usage.overview: Specific guidance for using available tools effectively during i18n and translation tasks. This guide helps you choose the right tool for each step of the workflow.

priority.when: Start of any task - understand existing structure
priority.why: Essential context before making any changes

priority.when: Need to find all language files
priority.why: Discover all translation files that need attention

priority.when: Looking for specific keys or translation patterns
priority.why: Find how terms are translated elsewhere for consistency

priority.when: Making targeted edits to existing translations
priority.why: Surgical edits preserve formatting and are efficient

priority.when: Creating new language files or complete rewrites
priority.why: Best for new files or when restructuring entirely

tool.name: read_file
tool.purpose: Read translation files to understand structure and content

best_practices.practice: Always read the base language file (en.ts) first
Read target language files to understand current state
Use line ranges for large files to focus on specific sections
Read multiple related files to understand consistency

example.description: Read specific lines to check a section

important_notes.note: Read files one at a time - multiple file reads are disabled
Use line ranges efficiently for large files
Always confirm file structure before editing

tool.name: list_files
tool.purpose: Discover all language files in i18n directories

best_practices.practice: Use recursive listing to find all locales
Identify which languages are currently supported
Check for consistent file naming across languages

tool.name: search_files
tool.purpose: Find specific translation keys or patterns across files

best_practices.practice: Search for existing translations of similar terms
Verify key consistency across language files
Find all occurrences of a specific key
Use regex patterns to find translation patterns

when_to_use.scenario: Finding how a term was translated elsewhere
Checking if a key exists across all languages

example.description: Find a specific translation key in all files
Find English text that might not be translated

tool.name: apply_diff
tool.purpose: Make precise, targeted modifications to translation files

best_practices.practice: Use for surgical edits to existing translations
Make multiple changes in one diff when related
Always include accurate line numbers
Preserve exact spacing and indentation
Read file first to ensure exact content match

when_to_use.scenario: Adding new keys to existing sections

example.description: Add a new translation key to existing section
example.code: <apply_diff> <path>medialake_user_interface/src/i18n/locales/fr.ts</path> <diff> <<<<<<< SEARCH :start_line:100 ------- save: "Enregistrer", cancel: "Annuler", }, ======= save: "Enregistrer", cancel: "Annuler", delete: "Supprimer", }, >>>>>>> REPLACE </diff> </apply_diff>

<apply_diff> <path>medialake_user_interface/src/i18n/locales/de.ts</path> <diff> <<<<<<< SEARCH :start_line:50 ------- title: "Benutzer", ======= title: "Benutzerprofil", >>>>>>> REPLACE
<<<<<<< SEARCH :start_line:75 ------- save: "Speichern", ======= save: "Änderungen speichern", >>>>>>> REPLACE </diff> </apply_diff>

critical_rules.rule: SEARCH content must match file exactly, including whitespace
Use single ======= line between SEARCH and REPLACE
Read file first if uncertain about exact content
Multiple SEARCH/REPLACE blocks in one diff for efficiency

tool.name: write_to_file
tool.purpose: Create new translation files or perform complete file rewrites

best_practices.practice: Use for creating new language files
Provide complete file content - never truncate
Maintain consistent structure with other language files
Include proper export format
Count lines accurately

when_not_to_use.scenario: Adding single keys (use apply_diff or insert_content)
Files larger than a few hundred lines (use apply_diff)

example.description: Create a new Italian translation file
example.code:
<write_to_file>
<path>medialake_user_interface/src/i18n/locales/it.ts</path>
<content>
export default {
  languages: {
    english: "Inglese",
    italian: "Italiano",
  },
  common: {
    save: "Salva",
    cancel: "Annulla",
    delete: "Elimina",
  },
  // ... complete file content
};

tool.name: ask_followup_question
tool.purpose: Request clarification or additional information from user

best_practices.practice: Ask when translation context is ambiguous
Provide 2-4 specific, actionable suggestions
Make suggestions complete (no placeholders)
Order suggestions by likelihood or importance

when_to_use.scenario: Uncertain which language to translate to

example.description: Ask which language to translate to
example.code: <ask_followup_question> <question>Which language should I translate the i18n file to?</question> <follow_up> <suggest>Spanish (es.ts)</suggest> <suggest>French (fr.ts)</suggest> <suggest>German (de.ts)</suggest> <suggest>All available languages (es, fr, de, pt, zh, ja, ko, ar, he, hi)</suggest> </follow_up> </ask_followup_question>

<ask_followup_question> <question>The term "assets" appears in multiple contexts. How should it be translated in Spanish?</question> <follow_up> <suggest>Use "activos" (business/financial assets)</suggest> <suggest>Use "recursos" (media/digital resources)</suggest> <suggest>Use "archivos" (files/documents)</suggest> </follow_up> </ask_followup_question>

tool.name: execute_command
tool.purpose: Run commands for validation or testing

best_practices.practice: Validate TypeScript/JavaScript syntax
Run linters to check code quality
Test that files can be imported

sequence.name: complete_file_translation
sequence.description: Translate an entire i18n file to a new language

step.rationale: Understand complete structure and all keys

step.action: Create target language file with translations
step.rationale: Most efficient for complete new file

sequence.name: add_keys_to_all_languages
sequence.description: Add new translation keys across all language files

step.rationale: Identify all languages that need updates

step.action: Read each language file to find insertion point
step.rationale: Determine exact location for new keys

step.action: Add new keys to each language file

sequence.name: sync_missing_keys
sequence.description: Synchronize missing keys across language files

step.rationale: Get complete list of all keys

sequence.name: validate_translations
sequence.description: Check translation files for completeness and correctness

step.action: Read each file to check structure
Search for common issues (missing variables, HTML problems)
Find the keys that need updating

strategy.name: batch_similar_changes
strategy.description: Group similar changes into single tool uses
strategy.technique: Use multiple SEARCH/REPLACE blocks in one apply_diff
strategy.benefit: Reduces number of tool calls and file operations

strategy.name: read_once_edit_many
strategy.description: Read file once, plan all changes, make them together
strategy.technique: Analyze entire file before making any edits

strategy.name: pattern_reuse
strategy.description: Use search_files to find existing translation patterns
strategy.technique: Search for similar keys to maintain consistency

strategy.name: validate_early
strategy.technique: Check file syntax and structure at start
strategy.benefit: Avoid wasted effort on malformed files

mistake.name: not_reading_first
mistake.description: Making changes without reading file first
mistake.consequence: Incorrect line numbers, wrong content in SEARCH blocks
mistake.solution: Always read file before using apply_diff

mistake.name: inexact_search
mistake.description: SEARCH block doesn't match file exactly

causes.cause: File was modified since last read

solutions.solution: Read file again to get current content
Copy exact content for SEARCH block

issue.name: syntax_error_after_change
issue.symptoms: File has syntax errors after modification

solutions.solution: Use execute_command to run syntax checker
Read file to identify error location
Verify export format matches other files
Check import statements in main i18n config

performance_tips.tip: Read files strategically - don't re-read unnecessarily
Combine related edits into single apply_diff
Use search_files to quickly locate keys across multiple files
Plan all changes before starting edits
Use line ranges to read only needed sections of large files
Validate once at end rather than after each change
